suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update rust-library-worker guidance for table_as work to explicitly require auditing macro-generated identity and hydration seams (for example HasId, ResolveRecordId, and Bridge dispatch) alongside repository/runtime paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Initial review of milestone-f-table-as-storage-identity found repository CRUD moved to storage_table(), but macros/src/lib.rs still used table_name() in generated explicit-id identity and enum bridge hydration paths until corrective feature milestone-f-table-as-storage-identity-macro-bridge-fix landed.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify in rust-library-worker that pure API-surface/documentation features may be validated with compile-surface proof plus docs/rustdoc alignment when no new runtime behavior is introduced.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of milestone-f-repo-public-internal-api-refactor passed based on README/rustdoc alignment, generated inherent methods, and facade compile-surface coverage, but current skill text does not state this validation pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document a Windows-safe fallback for jq-style read-only mission inspection steps (for example Python) in scrutiny/rust-library worker guidance when examples currently assume jq availability.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both the table-as macro fix review and the original worker transcript had to infer a Python fallback because skill examples reference jq while this Windows environment does not guarantee jq.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Define how mission state should record proof ownership when a validation-matrix feature is satisfied by already-landed sibling commits rather than a dedicated runtime commit, including the evidence-only corrective-feature pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The original milestone-f-focused-regression-matrix-separation review failed because there was no feature-owned artifact; the corrective evidence feature repaired this by adding an ownership artifact and mission-state reconciliation, but the general convention is still undocumented.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Allow evidence-only corrective features to complete via validation artifact plus mission-state reconciliation when their purpose is ownership repair rather than new runtime behavior.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review of milestone-f-focused-regression-matrix-separation-evidence passed based on an evidence artifact and mission metadata updates rather than Rust code changes, but rust-library-worker procedure still assumes implementation features primarily add runtime code or tests.
